name: Backpack (Standard or Halfling)
desc: A Backpack will hold a maximum 40 pounds or 3
cubic feet of goods. Some items may be lashed to the
outside, and thus count toward the weight limit but not
the volume limit. A Halfling's backpack holds at most 30
pounds and/or 1½ cubic feet, but costs the same as a

name: Belt Pouch
desc: Belt Pouches are small cloth or leather sacks
that are commonly fastened to a belt or bandolier. They
are useful for carrying small items close to hand, such
as spell components, lockpicks, or a pipe and tobacco.

name: Bit and bridle
desc: A Bit and Bridle is a combination item that is
held in a horse's or pony's mouth to guide them while

name: Candles, 12
desc: A Candle will shed light over a 5' radius, with
dim light extending 5' further. A normal candle will
burn about 3 turns per inch of height.

name: Chalk, small bag of pieces
desc: Chalk is useful for 'blazing a trail' through a dungeon

name: Cloak
desc: A cloak is an outer garment similar to a cape, but
larger and heavier, designed to provide protection from
the elements. It often includes either an integrated or

name: Clothing, common outfit
desc: A full set of clothing typical of your average

name: Glass bottle or vial
desc: A Bottle is a small glass container suitable for
filling with a potion, holy water, or other liquid you

name: Grappling Hook
desc: A Grappling Hook is a device made of iron or
steel resembling a claw, or sometimes a miniature three
-tined anchor. It is tied to the end of a rope and then
The purpose is to secure the hook to an outcrop of some
kind, allowing the rope to be used for climbing to that

name: Holy Symbol
desc: Holy Symbols come in as many varieites as
there are religions. While they all have
as well. While anyone can own a holy symbol
about them. In the hands of a true cleric,
however, they become the focus for their
spells and the primary tool for turning or

name: Holy Water, per vial
desc: Holy Water is harmful to undead creatures. A
character may throw a flask of holy water as a
grenade-like missile, which will break and release the
contents if thrown against the body of a corporeal
creature. To use it against an incorporeal creature it
must be opened and poured or splashed onto the target,
generally requiring the attacker to be directly adjacent
Each flask of holy water can inflict 1d8 points of
damage to such a monster. In addition, each additional
undead monster within 5 feet of the point of impact
receives 1d6 points of damage from the splash. Holy
Water is only effective for one round.

name: Horseshoes & shoeing
desc: Horseshoes are U-shaped pieces of iron that are
nailed to the bottom of a horse's (or pony's) hooves in
order to protect them from injury and excessive wear.

name: Ink, per jar
desc: Ink is primarily used for writing letters and
journals or for making maps.  It usually requires a
Quill pen or a small brush to use it properly.

name: Iron Spikes, 12
desc: Iron Spikes are useful for spiking doors closed
(or spiking them open) and may be used as crude pitons

name: Ladder, 10 ft.
desc: A ladder is a device used to climb things. It is
generally constructed with two long poles standing
parallel to each other and joined together by several
horizontal pieces called rungs. The ladder is then
leaned against whatever is to be climbed, such as a wall
or tree, and the rungs are used as steps to ascend.

name: Lantern
desc: A Lantern will provide light covering a 30'
radius; dim light will extend about 20' further. A
lantern will consume a flask of oil in 18+1d6 turns.

name: Lantern, Bullseye
desc: A Bullseye Lantern projects a cone of light 30'
long and 30' wide at the widest point, with dim light
extending an additional 20' beyond that point. This type

name: Lantern, Hooded
desc: A Hooded Lantern allows the light to be hidden
or revealed as the user pleases; in all other ways it

name: Manacles (without padlock)
desc: Manacles normally consist of a pair of hinged
iron or steel cuffs that can be placed over the wrists
of a medium-sized humanoid and locked in place with one

name: Map or scroll case
desc: A Map or Scroll Case is a tubular oiled leather
case used to carry maps, scrolls, or other paper items.
The case will have a water-resistant (but not waterproof)
cap which slides over the end and a loop to allow the
case to be hung from a belt or bandolier. A standard
scroll case can hold up to 10 sheets of paper, or a
single scroll of up to seven spells.

name: Mirror, small metal
desc: A Mirror is useful in a dungeon environment for
many reasons; for instance, it is the only way to look
at a Medusa without being turned to stone. Mirrors are
also useful for looking around corners, and can be used
outdoors to send signals using reflected sunlight.

name: Oil (per flask)
desc: Flasks of Oil are primarily used to keep lanterns
fueled. A flask contains enough oil to keep a lantern
Oil flasks can also be thrown like vials of holy water,
coating the target with a layer of oil which can then be
lit, doing 1d8 fire damage for 2 rounds. The most common
method is by throwing a lit torch into the oil, but

name: Padlock (with 2 keys)
desc: Padlocks are metal devices used to secure things,
such as chests, doors or manacles. Padlocks usually come
with 2 keys, but may also be picked if necessary.

name: Paper (per sheet)
desc: Each sheet of paper weighs virtually nothing.

name: Quill
desc: A Quill is the Medieval equivalent of a
ballpoint pen. If you dip it in some ink,

name: Quill Knife
desc: A Quill Knife keeps your quill points pointy!
And yes, Clerics can use them!

name: Quiver or Bolt case
desc: A Quiver is an open container used to hold
arrows. A Bolt Case is a similar sort of
container for crossbow bolts. In either case, the
standard capacity is 20 missiles. The length of a quiver
or bolt case must match the length of the ammunition
for it to be useful; therefore, there are longbow and
shortbow quivers and light and heavy crossbow bolt
cases. The price is the same for all types.

name: Rations, Dry, one week
desc: Dry Rations may consist of dry bread, hard
cheese, dried fruit, nuts, beans, jerky, or any other
food which will not 'go bad' in less than about a
month (if not longer). Dry rations are generally sold
in quantities sufficient for one character for a week,
and are packaged in waxed or oiled cloth to protect them.

name: Rope, Hemp (per 50 ft.)
desc: Hemp Rope is one-half inch in diameter and has a
breaking strength of 1,600 pounds. Safe working load
for a rope is normally one-quarter of the breaking
strength. One or more knots in a rope cut the breaking
strength in half. This does not affect the safe working
load, because knots are figured into the listed one-

name: Rope, Silk (per 50 ft.)
desc: Silk Rope is about three-eighths inch in
diameter and has a breaking strength of 1,600 pounds,
although it weighs considerably less than hemp rope.
The notes regarding rope strength given for hemp rope

name: Sack, Large
desc: A Large Sack will hold at most 40 pounds or 4

name: Sack, Small
desc: A Small Sack will hold at most 20 pounds or 2

name: Saddle, Pack
desc: A Pack Saddle is any device designed to be
secured on the back of a horse, mule, or other working
animal so it can carry heavy loads such as luggage,
firewood or other things too heavy to be carried by

name: Saddle, Riding
desc: A Riding Saddle is a device designed to be
fastened onto the back of a mount that enables a
person to ride on the animal's back.

name: Saddlebags, Pair
desc: A pair of Saddlebags will hold at most 10
pounds or 1 cubic foot of goods (divided evenly between

name: Spellbook (128 pages)
desc: A Spellbook is a magic-user's most important
possession. Part research journal, part reference
book, a spellbook contains the spells they have learned,
including materials. incantations and gestures needed to
cast each spell. The higher the level of spell, the more
pages it tends to take up, requiring a magic user to
acquire more tomes as they progress in knowledge and

name: Tent, Large (ten men)
desc: A Tent is a temporary structure made up of a
heavy fabric, such as canvas, wrapped around a wooden
frame and secured with thin ropes. The fabric is usually
treated in order to be waterproof, or at least water-
This tent is sized to accomodate ten people at once.

name: Tent, Small (one man)
desc: A Tent is a temporary structure made up of a
heavy fabric, such as canvas, wrapped around a wooden
frame and secured with thin ropes. The fabric is usually
treated in order to be waterproof, or at least water-
This tent is sized to accomodate one person.

name: Thieves' Picks and Tools
desc: Thieves' Picks and Tools are required for the use
of Thief abilities such as opening locks and removing
traps. These abilities may not be usable without
appropriate tools, or may be used at a penalty at the

name: Tinderbox, Flint and Steel
desc: A Tinderbox is generally purchased with a
; the flint, a piece of hard rock,
is struck vigorously against a C-shaped piece of high-
carbon steel. When done correctly, hot sparks will fly
from the flint and steel into the tinder, hopefully
The best tinder is a dried piece of prepared tinder
fungus, carried in the tinderbox to keep it dry; char
cloth, hemp rope, or even very dry grass can substitute
if prepared tinder fungus is not available. The time
required to start a fire should be determined by the GM
according to the prevailing conditions; under ideal
conditions, starting a fire with a flint, steel and

name: Torches, 6
desc: A Torch sheds light over a 30' radius, with dim
light extending about 20' further, and burns for 1d4+4
turns. Of course, a torch is also useful for setting
flammable materials (such as cobwebs or oil) alight.

name: Whetstone
desc: A Whetstone is used to sharpen and maintain
edged weapons such as swords, daggers, and axes.

name: Whistle
desc: A Whistle is a mouth-operated device that makes
a high-pitched noise in order to indicate a signal. The
situations where such a device is most useful are ones
in which speedy communication is needed between people

name: Wineskin/Waterskin
desc: A Wineskin(or Waterskin) is a container for
drinking wine(or water); though generally water is
taken into a dungeon or wilderness environment. The
standard waterskin holds one quart of liquid, which is
the minimum amount required by a normal character in a
single day. If adventuring in the desert or other hot,
dry areas, a character may need as much as ten times
this amount. Note that the given 2 pound weight is for a
full skin; an empty skin has negligible weight.

name: Winter Blanket
desc: A Winter Blanket is simply a blanket designed to
keep a person warm in winter, especially when camping.
It is often made of wool, but its defining feature is
its greater thickness and weight over standard blankets.
Many adventurers buy winter blankets simply for use as
mattresses while in the wilderness or dungeon.

name: Hand Axe
desc: A Hand Axe is a simple one-handed weapon. It

name: Battle Axe
desc: A Battle Axe is a one-handed weapon larger than
a simple hand axe, specialized for combat.

name: Great Axe
desc: A Great Axe is a large, two-handed weapon. It
does a significant amount of damage, and can normally
only be wielded by Medium or Large creatures.
EXECEPTION: Dwarves can wield Great Axes, but no other

name: Shortbow
desc: A > is a simple bow that can be fired
from horseback by Medium sized or larger creatures.

name: Shortbow Arrow
desc: Shortbow arrows are designed to be fired from a

name: Silver Shortbow Arrow
desc: The heads of Silver Shortbow Arrows have been
coated with silver, allowing them to damage lycanthropes

name: Longbow
desc: A Longbow is larger and has more range than a
Shortbow. It typically requires the user to be standing
while firing. It cannot be wielded by creature smaller

name: Longbow Arrow
desc: Longbow Arrows are designed to be fired from a

name: Silver Longbow Arrow
desc: The heads of Silver Longbow Arrows have been
coated with silver, allowing them to damage lycanthropes

name: Light Crossbow
desc: Light Crossbows are simple, handheld devices that
use a mechanical assembly to fire bolts. They are easy to
wield and require less strength to operate compared to
Light crossbows offer superior accuracy and power over
short to mid-range distances. They are favored by
adventurers who value reliability and ease of use.

name: Light Quarrel
desc: Light Quarrels are bolts designed to be fired
from a Light Crossbow. 10 of them weigh 1 pound.

name: Silver Light Quarrel
desc: The heads of these <b>Silver Light Quarrels have been
coated with silver, allowing them to damage lycanthropes

name: Heavy Crossbow
desc: Heavy Crossbows are more advanced versions of
Light Crossbows, featuring a larger frame and increased
Heavy Crossbows require more strength to handle
effectively but they have greater piercing power and
Heavy Crossbows are ideal for adventurers who want to
deliver devastating shots to their enemies.

name: Heavy Quarrel
desc: Heavy Quarrels are bolts designed to be fired
from a Light Crossbow. 10 of them weigh 1 pound.

name: Silver Heavy Quarrel
desc: The heads of these Silver Heavy Quarrels have
been coated with silver, allowing them to damage

name: Dagger
desc: A Dagger is a versatile weapon used in close-
quarters combat and short ranged missle combat. They
are light, easy to conceal and can be thrown with
They are popular with Thieves for swift and silent
kills, with Fighters as a last resort, and with Magic

name: Longsword / Scimitar
desc: be wielded with one hand by
however they are curved to a

name: Two-Handed Sword
desc: While their size can be un

name: Mace
desc: iron or steel on the end of a

name: Club/Cudgel/Walking Staff
desc: the way of training and are

name: Pole Arm
desc: ing the ability to hit oppon
in front of the wielder, and to strike first against

name: Sling
desc: A Sling is a projectile weapon typically used
to hand-throw a blunt projectile such as a stone or lead
A sling has a small pouch in the middle of two cords,
where a projectile is placed. There is a loop on the end
of one of the cords and a tab on the end of the other.
Depending on the design, either the middle finger or the
wrist is placed through the loop and the tab is placed
between the thumb and forefinger. The sling is swung in
an arc, and the tab released at a precise moment,
causing the projectile to fly towards the target.

name: Sling Bullet
desc: Sling Bullets are small metal balls made of lead
or iron, sized to fit within the pouch of a sling.

name: Sling Stone
desc: Sling Stones are small round stones, sized to fit
within the pouch of a sling.

name: Spear
desc: A Spear is an interstingly versatile weapon. It
can be wielded in melee combat either one- or two-handed,
doing 1d6 or 1d8 damage respectivly, or it can be thrown,
This gives a melee fighter a choice between using a spear
with one hand, allowing them to use a shield for greater
defense and lesser damage or two-handed, for lesser

name: Leather
desc: leather.The breastplate and shoulder sections consist of
stiffer, thicker pieces that have been boiled in oil. The
rest of the armor is made of softer pieces that allow
This armor is especially prized by thieves, since it
provides defense and allows silent movement.

name: Chain Mail
desc: Chain Mail is constructed from hundreds, if not
thousands of interlocking metal rings.  The most common
type is a shirt of mail worn over a padded garment known
as a gambeson. Chain mail commonly has an attached hood
of rings to protect the head and neck.

name: Plate Mail
desc: The ultimate in armored protection, Plate Mail is
made from plates of metal that are fitted over arms,
legs and torso, supplemented with portions of chain mail.
Plate armor includes gaunlets and a helmet.
Plate mail must be custom-fitted to the wearer. Although,
if a new owner is not too differently sized, it can
usually be adjusted by an armorsmith.

name: Shield
desc: A Shield is a defensive device held in a melee
fighter's off hand. It is the 'board' part of the
fighting style known as 'Sword and Board'. However, a
shield can be employed with any one-handed weapon.
Shields are commonly made of split-resistant wood such as
poplar and are often reinforced with metal, covered with
leather or both. Shields made entirely of metal exist,

name: Horse, Draft
desc: A <b>Draft Horse<b/> is a large type of horse bred for
strength over speed.  Teams of draft horses are capable
of pulling heavy loads steadily over long distances.
While these horses can be ridden, they will not be able
to maintain the more rapid pace of a riding horse.

name: Horse, War
desc: A War Horse is a riding horse that has been bred
and trained to fight in combat. By using particular leg
movements and verbal commands, a war horse will fight
either by itself or in tandem with its mounted rider.
A war horse can bite, kick to the rear and (due to their
more powerful hindquarters) kick to the front by rearing,

name: Horse, Riding
desc: A Riding Horse, in contrast to a draft horse,
has been bred for speed over strength. Riding horses
are employed most often as personal mounts.  While
riding horses can be outfitted with pack saddles and/or
saddlebags, they cannot transport goods as effectively
as a team of draft horses with a wagon.

name: Pony
desc: Ponies are a smaller breed of horse, sturdier
than their larger cousins.  Ponies are often the favored
mounts of dwarves and halflings, since their smaller
stature makes them easier for these races to manage.

name: Pony, War
desc: A War Pony is simply a pony that has been
trained to respond to partcular leg movements and
verbal commands, allowing its rider to fight while
mounted. They can also fight if they are not being
War ponies can deliver a bite attack, but their rear
kicks, while capable of knocking someone prone, are not
